questions.question: What's the #1 weighted criterion for solo founders choosing an outreach platform?
questions.explanation: Deliverability is weighted 10/10 because if your emails don't land in the inbox, nothing else matters. Price is #2, but a cheap tool that gets you spam-foldered is worthless.

questions.question: For a solo founder selling $500/month SaaS to small businesses, which platform makes the most sense?

options.text: La Growth Machine Pro ($100/mo) — email + LinkedIn + Twitter

questions.explanation: Low ACV ($500/mo) + transactional sale = email-only is sufficient. Multi-channel ROI won't justify the extra $40-60/month cost. Instantly gives you everything you need at the lowest price.

questions.question: What's the hidden cost of switching outreach platforms mid-campaign?

options.text: Just the new platform's monthly fee
8-12 hours of setup + 2 weeks of warmup disruption
You lose all your contact data

questions.explanation: Every migration costs 8-12 hours of reconfiguration plus 2 weeks where your sending volume drops to zero during warmup. This compounds if you switch multiple times.

questions.question: When does multi-channel outreach justify its 60-100% higher cost?

options.text: When ACV is >$5K and the sales cycle is relationship-first

questions.explanation: Multi-channel adds 20-30% incremental pipeline but costs 60-100% more. The math works when ACV is high enough ($5K+) and the sale requires relationship-building. For low-ticket or transactional sales, email-only wins.

questions.question: What's the main advantage of Instantly over Smartlead for most solo founders?

options.text: Cleaner UI and better A/B testing (up to 26 variants)

questions.explanation: Instantly has a simpler UI and superior A/B testing (26 variants vs Smartlead's more complex setup). Smartlead wins on API/webhooks, but 90% of solo founders don't need that. Instantly is $2/month cheaper and easier to learn.

questions.question: True or False: You should switch platforms if you find one with 10% better features.
questions.explanation: False. The switching cost (8-12 hours + 2 weeks warmup disruption) outweighs a 10% feature improvement. Only switch if the current platform has a critical flaw (deliverability issues, missing must-have feature, unsustainable cost).

questions.question: What's the realistic total monthly cost for a solo founder outreach stack (platform + domains + enrichment)?
questions.explanation: Platform ($37-150) + domains/hosting ($30-45) + enrichment ($0-99) = $79-200/month for a functional stack. Under $79 is possible but requires free tiers and manual work. Over $200 is overkill for solo founders.

questions.question: According to the 'Good Enough' principle, what should you prioritize when choosing a platform?

options.text: The platform with the most features
The platform your favorite guru recommends
A platform that meets 5 core criteria and you'll use consistently

questions.explanation: The 'Good Enough' test: (1) Reliable deliverability, (2) A/B testing, (3) Tracking, (4) Under budget, (5) Usable UI. If it passes all 5, pick it and commit for 90 days. Consistency beats feature-chasing.
